name: Infleqtion, Inc.
business: Infleqtion develops and commercializes quantum technology products including quantum sensing, quantum computing, and quantum software. It generates revenue through product sales, quantum computing as a service, government-funded R&D contracts, and software licensing. Its customers include government agencies, defense prime contractors, research institutions, and commercial enterprises.
namedCustomers: J.P. Morgan Chase Global Technology Applied Research Team

exposures.sentence: We are developing and commercializing quantum technology products as part of a full-stack platform, which currently includes offerings such as quantum sensing, quantum computing and software.
Our quantum sensing portfolio, including Tiqker (optical atomic clocks), Sqywire (RF sensing) and Exaqt (inertial/gravitational sensing)
Our quantum-enabled solutions are focused on addressing the world s most pressing challenges, with technologies actively deployed across a number of sectors today, including defense and security
We are also pioneering the development of next-generation quantum inertial and gravimetric sensors for GPS-denied environments, including in space and underwater.
including defense and security, artificial intelligence (AI), energy optimization, space and frontier, materials discovery and cybersecurity.
In addition, we have developed CML software, based on quantum physics principles, that significantly amplifies AI performance today on classical graphics processing units (GPUs).

Superstaq enables hardware-aware optimization, supports hybrid quantum-classical workflows and integrates into enterprise and high-performance computing environments via application programming interfaces.
These systems are already being used to synchronize timing across networks
we are uniquely positioned to serve the growing market for space-based quantum technologies because our atom neutral platform has already been demonstrated in orbit
We maintain collaborative development and go-to-market relationships with defense prime contractors including SAIC, L3Harris, Safran and QinetiQ.

The global quantum computing market is expected to reach approximately $130 billion by 2040, as adoption expands across materials science, energy systems, financial services, artificial intelligence, cybersecurity, logistics optimization and pharmaceuticals.

catalysts.sentence: We have demonstrated 12 logical qubits and are aiming to reach 30 logical qubits in 2026.
Our quantum computing roadmap targets achieving 100 logical qubits by 2028.
We also target demonstrating MegaQuOp scale in 2028, measuring one million sequential logical operations in a system.

tamClaims.claim: Global quantum sensing market (including timing) projected to reach approximately $30 billion by 2040.
tamClaims.sentence: According to third-party industry forecasts, the global quantum sensing market (including timing) is projected to reach approximately $30 billion by 2040, with primary use cases in precision timing, navigation, gravity measurement and RF field detection.
